name: Marcus Tilden
education: High school diploma, 3 vocational certifications
personal_stake: His small gunsmith YouTube channel (180K subscribers) monetizes tutorials for legal firearm maintenance, but he's noticed ISP throttling during upload times. He needs reliable internet to compete with larger channels that likely have premium bandwidth deals.
quirk: Spends 6 weeks every fall hunting elk in Colorado, documents entire trips on a hand-crank satellite messenger because he distrusts cell networks

name: Ruth Feldman
occupation: Retired archivist and unpublished memoir writer
education: Master's degree in Library Science
personal_stake: She's trying to self-publish her mother's Holocaust testimony online and needs affordable bandwidth to upload high-resolution photographs and video testimonials; she fears ISPs could throttle nonprofit historical archives.
quirk: Spends 15 hours per week training therapy dogs for nursing homes despite arthritis, and names each one after a relative who perished in the war

name: Marcus Delgado
education: High school diploma + apprenticeship certification
personal_stake: His union uses encrypted messaging apps and livestreams to coordinate job site safety complaints and wage negotiations—throttling could cripple organizing efforts. He's also helping his 16-year-old daughter apply to colleges online from their single internet connection.
quirk: Spends weekends restoring vintage radio equipment and has a 2,000-person Discord of ham radio enthusiasts he moderates

name: Marcus DeVries
occupation: independent hip-hop artist / part-time medical coder
education: high school diploma + online certification in healthcare IT
personal_stake: His music uploads get throttled on slower Chicago South Side connections while wealthy North Shore artists' content streams flawlessly. He's seen firsthand how ISPs prioritize rich neighborhoods, making it nearly impossible for independent artists like him to compete without paying for prioritization.
quirk: volunteers as a free coder for a rare disease patient registry while simultaneously running a side hustle selling custom beat-making samples on Discord

name: Mira Chen-Rodriguez
occupation: Independent sex worker and part-time medical coder
education: Some college (online coding bootcamp, no degree)
personal_stake: Payment processors routinely throttle or block transactions for sex work-adjacent services, forcing her into slower payment gateways that cost more. Net neutrality violations could let ISPs do the same discrimination at the network layer, making it even harder for marginalized workers to access banking infrastructure.
quirk: Maintains a detailed spreadsheet analyzing payment processor fees across 47 platforms and teaches financial literacy workshops to other sex workers entirely off-the-books

name: Marcus Chen
occupation: Professional Twitch streamer (competitive FPS) / Part-time network infrastructure consultant
education: Bachelor's in Physics; self-taught networking through trial and error
personal_stake: My income depends on streaming 40+ hours weekly with zero latency tolerance—any ISP throttling of gaming traffic directly cuts my earnings and viewer count. I've also spent $12K upgrading my home network because providers won't guarantee fair bandwidth allocation.
quirk: Collects vintage networking equipment from the 1990s and restores them as a side hobby; has a functioning Cisco router from 1998 on his desk as a conversation piece

name: Marcus Chen-Williams
occupation: Aspiring rapper / part-time network technician at community college
education: High school diploma, currently enrolled in 2-year IT cert program
personal_stake: ISPs throttling video uploads kill his TikTok momentum during peak engagement hours; he's also seen his South Side neighborhood get charged premium rates for 'prioritized' streaming while wealthier zip codes get standard speeds cheaper.
quirk: Obsessively studies 1980s telephone monopoly history as a hobby and frequently compares ISP behavior to Ma Bell in his friend group

name: Margaret Chen-Rousseau
occupation: Reference librarian & senior tech literacy instructor
education: MLS from University of Michigan, BA in Comparative Literature
personal_stake: My 73-year-old students finally have affordable access to telehealth and online banking through their library accounts, but I'm terrified ISPs will throttle health sites or charge seniors extra. I've watched too many of them choose between medication and internet.
quirk: Collects vintage computer manuals from estate sales and has bound them into an art installation called 'The Obsolescence Garden' in her home

name: Marcus Whitfield
education: High school diploma + 2 years community college (incomplete)
personal_stake: His 16-year-old daughter uses YouTube for free coding tutorials because he can't afford paid courses; he's terrified ISPs will throttle educational content or charge extra, locking poor kids like her out of upward mobility.
quirk: Maintains a brutally honest recovery blog with 2,000 followers where he documents job struggles post-addiction; has become a minor voice in Appalachian blue-collar recovery circles.

name: Raymond Hutchins
occupation: Methodist pastor (part-time); substitute teacher; hobby beekeeping consultant
education: Master of Divinity from DePauw, undergraduate in agricultural science
personal_stake: My rural congregation of 43 people relies on affordable broadband for Sunday livestreams to reach shut-ins, and our local school district—where I substitute teach—can't afford ISP throttling of educational video content. Without net neutrality, we're priced out entirely.
quirk: Maintains three Facebook pages (one official church page he doesn't understand, one for his beekeeping mentorship group, one secret page where he posts philosophical complaints about church decline under a pseudonym)

name: Ruth Fineman
occupation: Retired Yiddish translator; unpublished memoir writer
education: BA Comparative Literature (1972); self-taught in digital humanities
personal_stake: Her memoir documenting her mother's survival stories exists only on a shared Google Drive because she can't afford faster internet—and she's terrified an ISP slowdown will delete years of work or make cloud backup impossible for people like her on fixed incomes.
quirk: Obsessively records oral histories of elderly Yiddish speakers in her building using a 2009 Flip camera, worried the language will vanish; has 847 unlabeled video files she's trying to organize before she dies.

name: Marcus Chen-Patterson
occupation: Podcast producer and criminal justice reform advocate
education: BA in Music Composition, self-taught in audio engineering
personal_stake: His podcast reaches incarcerated listeners through prison library systems via paid partnerships, and ISP throttling of educational content disproportionately affects the low-bandwidth environments where his audience lives. He's seen firsthand how differential pricing creates information deserts in corrections facilities.
quirk: Competes semi-professionally in competitive pickling—has won three regional fermentation contests and uses fermentation metaphors to explain complex systems to non-technical audiences.

name: Derek Castellanos
education: Currently pursuing B.S. in Marine Biology, UC Santa Barbara; took AP Comp Sci in high school
personal_stake: Uses low-bandwidth scientific data repositories and open-access journals for research on kelp forest ecosystems; fears ISP throttling of academic content would price out students from less-funded labs and make reproducible science impossible for his generation.
quirk: Competes in competitive pinball tournaments on vintage machines and runs a niche blog comparing pinball machine mechanics to predator-prey dynamics in marine ecosystems

name: Marcus Webb
education: Some college (criminal justice coursework, no degree)
personal_stake: His deaf wife relies on video relay services and ASL interpretation apps that require consistent, fast broadband—throttling could make real-time communication impossible. He's also frustrated watching inmates in his facility have unequal access to education resources based on ISP speed.
quirk: He's a competitive competitive eater who trains for hot dog contests on weekends; his wife manages his social media accounts and trash-talks opponents in ASL for his followers.

name: Sadie Kowalski
education: BS Mathematics, completed actuarial exams 1 and 2
personal_stake: Her kids rely on heavily-compressed Khan Academy and open-source STEM resources that wouldn't survive paid prioritization; her actuarial work requires real-time data access without artificial throttling, which subsidizes their household during her husband's 18-month deployment.
quirk: Obsessively collects vintage ham radio equipment and teaches her oldest child morse code as a 'digital independence' project.

name: Margaret Chen-Rothschild
occupation: Organic avocado farmer & unpaid IT support for rural seniors
education: High school diploma, self-taught coder (1980s), attended 3 months of community college business
personal_stake: My farm's water management system and emergency backup internet rely on affordable connectivity to rural areas—I've seen ISPs throttle agricultural data traffic. My neighbor's grandson can't access telemedicine appointments in our area because of speed tiers that favor wealthy towns.
quirk: She runs an unlicensed dial-up BBS out of her barn (still active, 7 users) and believes 5G towers interfere with soil microbiome but thinks net neutrality is essential because 'big telecom is worse than anything the government does'

name: Marcus Chen-Okafor
occupation: Tenant rights organizer / part-time mycologist consultant
education: BA Environmental Science, community college diploma in urban ecology
personal_stake: His tenant organizing network relies on cheap, reliable internet access to coordinate across fragmented neighborhoods. He's seen ISPs throttle video calls during rent strike planning sessions and fears tiered pricing will eliminate poor communities' ability to organize at scale.
quirk: Breeds lion's mane mushrooms in his apartment closet using coffee grounds from local cafes; sells them to restaurants under the table

name: Marcus Delgado
education: High school diploma + apprenticeship certification, currently auditing community college philosophy courses
personal_stake: His union negotiates remote apprenticeship curriculum through streaming video—if ISPs throttle union bandwidth, it directly undermines the apprenticeship program he's helping build for Dorchester kids. He also moonlights fixing internet infrastructure in public housing complexes.
quirk: Obsessed with the history of utility regulation; has read every major FCC decision since 1934 and maintains a hand-annotated physical filing system despite owning a laptop

name: Marcus Webb
occupation: Fly fishing guide and Instagram content creator
education: Some college (wildlife biology, incomplete)
personal_stake: My guided trips are booked entirely through Instagram—clients find me through video reels of sunset catches. Throttled video speeds or ISP preferencing would cripple my ability to upload content fast enough to stay visible, directly killing my $80k annual income.
quirk: Competes in regional fly-tying championships and has won twice; keeps detailed journals about insect hatches dating back 34 years that he's now digitizing

name: Marcus Weintraub
education: PhD Economics (University of Chicago), minor in Philosophy
personal_stake: His 16-year-old daughter was rejected from a competitive STEM summer program because her rural Michigan school's internet couldn't handle the application video upload—he sees net neutrality as essential market infrastructure, not regulation.
quirk: Spends weekends restoring 1980s ham radios and has a licensed amateur radio operator license; views the internet as a successor to that ecosystem.

name: Marcus Webb
education: High School Diploma + Vocational Locksmithing Certification
personal_stake: His remote elk hunting operation in Colorado depends on reliable internet access to run booking systems, live-stream hunts for clients, and manage supply chain orders. He's watched ISPs prioritize urban markets while rural broadband stagnates—seeing net neutrality as a fairness issue, not a liberal talking point.
quirk: Competes in international lockpicking sport competitions and has won regional championships; keeps detailed journals of every elk he's hunted since 1994.

name: Marcus Webb
education: BS Chemistry, MEd Curriculum Design
personal_stake: He streams his lab demonstrations on YouTube for homeschooled kids nationwide (200K subscribers), and he's terrified that ISPs could throttle educational content or demand fees that would price out rural families. His side income from Patreon—about $800/month—would evaporate if rural bandwidth gets tiered.
quirk: Competes seriously in ballroom dancing competitions on weekends; has placed top-10 regionally in Standard division.

name: Marcus Webb
education: BA in philosophy, no formal tech training
personal_stake: His podcast reaches incarcerated listeners via prison WiFi systems that ISPs throttle during peak hours, silencing voices he's amplified for five years. He's also a semi-professional competitive eater and worries ISPs will eventually charge extra for live-streaming his competitions.
quirk: Collects vintage prison mugshots from the 1960s-70s and has decorated his entire apartment with them; trains pigeons as a side hobby to deliver USB drives between DC office buildings

name: Maya Chen-Rodriguez
education: Some college (2 years), self-taught in digital art
personal_stake: Fast, unrestricted internet is essential for uploading large illustration files to clients and competing with established studios. Slower speeds for creative work would directly impact her ability to earn income as a freelancer while living on barista wages.
quirk: Competes in amateur competitive eating contests (holds a local record for Vietnamese food) and documents each event in a meticulously illustrated blog that gets moderate engagement

name: Kai Mendez
occupation: College student (marine biology major); part-time aquarium technician
education: Junior at UC Santa Barbara; high school valedictorian from rural Montana
personal_stake: My research on kelp forest disease requires downloading massive satellite imagery datasets that my university's internet can barely handle—if ISPs can throttle research traffic, marine conservation science becomes a pay-to-play game only rich institutions can afford.
quirk: Competes in competitive fly-fishing tournaments on weekends despite living near the ocean; grew up without internet until age 14 in a ranch town near Missoula

name: Miriam Hartvigsen
occupation: Etsy shop owner (handmade quilting patterns and digital downloads)
education: BFA in Fiber Arts, community college accounting certificate
personal_stake: Her Etsy income subsidizes her family's healthcare costs; she's watched her upload speeds degrade over two years and fears ISPs could throttle her competitors' sites while favoring big box retailers. She can't afford paid prioritization.
quirk: She's obsessed with 1970s mainframe aesthetics and incorporates pixelated computer punch-card patterns into her quilts; has a small but devoted collector base.

name: Marcus Whitfield
occupation: Organic avocado farmer and amateur radio enthusiast
education: High school diploma, extensive self-taught electronics
personal_stake: His farm's irrigation sensors and soil monitoring devices depend on consistent, affordable rural broadband that ISPs won't upgrade unless forced by neutrality rules. He also fears large tech companies using data throttling to eliminate small agricultural co-ops.
quirk: Builds and restores vintage ham radios from the 1960s-70s as a meditation practice and runs an unlicensed pirate radio station from his barn broadcasting organic farming tips and blues music to a three-mile radius.

name: Margaret Chen-Kowalski
occupation: Public librarian, senior tech literacy instructor
education: MLIS from University of Michigan, BA in Music Theory
personal_stake: My students—mostly people over 70 on fixed incomes—finally have affordable internet access through library programs. If ISPs throttle video content or create paid fast lanes, the telehealth and Medicare portal access I teach them will become unusable for anyone who can't pay extra.
quirk: Competes in regional speedcubing competitions (Rubik's cube solving) as stress relief; has personal best of 1:43

name: Derek Halloway
education: High school diploma, vocational marine biology certificate (2003)
personal_stake: His 16-year-old daughter uses online vocational courses to prepare for marine engineering while he's out on the boat, and he's terrified ISPs will throttle educational sites or charge more for them. He also relies on weather data and fishing reports that stream poorly on winter construction sites where he only has sketchy cellular.
quirk: Obsessively restores vintage nautical instruments (sextants, brass compasses, tide clocks) and has a small eBay business selling them, which requires reliable upload speeds to photograph items

name: Harpreet Singh Matharu
education: High school diploma, self-taught in networking and server maintenance
personal_stake: His truck stop relies on drivers streaming navigation, weather, and freight-matching apps simultaneously—expensive throttling by ISPs is cutting into his diner WiFi business and forcing him to pay ISP premiums that smaller competitors can't afford. He's seen three local mom-and-pop truck stops close in five years.
quirk: Maintains an amateur radio license (WB3KJL) and broadcasts hyperlocal trucking condition reports on CB band as a volunteer public service; has 40-year-old radio equipment he restored himself.

name: Marcus Delgado
education: BS Chemistry, MA Science Education
personal_stake: His students rely on YouTube chemistry tutorials and Khan Academy to supplement lab work, especially after budget cuts eliminated his prep periods. He's watched ISP throttling make these free resources load painfully slowly, creating an inequity gap between his students and wealthier suburban districts.
quirk: Competitive pickling enthusiast who ferments vegetables and sells jars at farmers markets under the name 'Delgado's Brine'—uses the income to fund his classroom's equipment since the school won't.

name: Margaret Chen-Dubois
occupation: Retired high school biology teacher, organic vegetable farmer
education: Master's degree in Environmental Science
personal_stake: Her farm's online CSA ordering system and direct-to-consumer sales depend on equal internet access to compete with larger distributors. She also mentors rural students remotely in science using video calls, which requires reliable, unthrottled bandwidth.
quirk: She breeds heritage chicken varieties as a side obsession and maintains an obscure poultry genetics forum with 200 members worldwide that would become financially unviable if ISPs could prioritize corporate agriculture sites.

name: Marcus Chen
education: MD from UCSF, additional fellowship in rural health informatics
personal_stake: I read CT scans for 7 rural hospitals in Appalachia and the Great Plains—if ISPs throttle medical imaging traffic or prioritize urban hospital networks, I could miss a stroke or tumor because pixels load too slowly. I've already seen one case delayed by bandwidth issues; it haunts me.
quirk: Competitive amateur beekeeper who uses machine learning to track hive health data; constantly worried about ISPs degrading his ability to upload sensor data to his predictive model

name: Marcus DeShawn Williams
occupation: Church organist (First Baptist Whitehaven) / Piano instructor
education: Some college (music conservatory, incomplete); self-taught in audio engineering
personal_stake: Streaming piano students' recitals and selling custom backing tracks online is throttled on certain ISPs, costing him ~$400/month in lost tutoring clients. His chronic back pain makes studio equipment setup critical—he can't afford redundant systems if speeds get degraded by paid prioritization.
quirk: Collects and restores vintage Hammond organs as a hobby; has 6 in various states of repair in his garage; believes the internet should work like a well-maintained pipe organ—every note reaches the listener with equal integrity.

name: Marcus Bellweather
occupation: Corn and soybean farmer (800 acres), part-time online commodities trader
education: Bachelor's in Agricultural Business, MIT (gap year before college; took online courses during farming seasons)
personal_stake: His commodity trading algorithm relies on real-time data feeds that require consistent bandwidth; he's watched rural ISPs throttle agricultural tech providers in favor of entertainment services, costing him thousands in missed market windows.
quirk: Competes in national-level online chess tournaments under an anonymous handle and has achieved Expert rating despite spending 14 hours daily on farm operations during growing season.

name: Derek Thibodeaux
education: High school diploma; enrolled in online AS (Business Administration) at Northeast Mississippi Community College
personal_stake: His online classes buffer constantly on the gas station WiFi because the ISP throttles video streaming after 50GB/month. Without net neutrality enforcement, he fears his provider will charge extra for 'education tier' access, making community college effectively unaffordable.
quirk: Restores vintage arcade machines as a side hobby (has a non-functional Pac-Man cabinet in his garage that he's slowly fixing); follows speedrunning communities obsessively.

name: Marcus Chen
education: High school diploma + 6 months completed at Western Nevada College (paused)
personal_stake: As a gig worker dependent on real-time app performance and unreliable home internet, I've watched delivery apps throttle during peak hours. If ISPs can create fast lanes, companies like DoorDash will pay for priority, making my already-thin margins disappear while I'm stuck with degraded service.
quirk: Builds and repairs vintage arcade machines as a side hobby; has restored three 1980s Pac-Man cabinets that he rents to bars, generating more stable income than some months of deliveries

name: Marcus Thorne
education: Some college (2 years, abandoned 1998)
personal_stake: My 16-year-old son uses YouTube to teach himself coding during slow shifts at my store on our WiFi—if ISPs throttle video, he loses his only pathway out of Appalachia. I've been sober 11 years and I see net neutrality like recovery: once you lose equal access, the system breaks.
quirk: Obsessive competitive bass fisherman who streams his catch to a niche Discord community of 200 people; spends more time troubleshooting streaming lag than managing inventory

name: Derek Matheson
education: High school diploma + maritime certification
personal_stake: His 16-year-old daughter uses online tutoring from a Portland university to stay in advanced courses—rural schools can't offer them—and relies on stable internet speeds. He's also angry that Comcast charges him $120/month for the same speeds city people pay $60 for.
quirk: Obsessively records weather patterns and marine life observations in a 20-year handwritten log that he's slowly digitizing; posts detailed phenology data to citizen science websites

name: Marcus Chen-Volkmann
education: BS Agricultural Economics, minor in Computer Science
personal_stake: His precision agriculture operation relies on real-time satellite data and IoT sensors for soil monitoring, which he uploads/downloads at variable speeds depending on ISP prioritization; a tiered internet would make his equipment subscriptions prohibitively expensive and put him at competitive disadvantage against larger operations with dedicated fiber.
quirk: Competes in competitive drone racing on weekends and streams the races—his secondary income comes from sponsorships, which depend on reliable upload speeds.

name: Frank Kowalski
occupation: Retired union electrician, part-time Tesla Supercharger network technician
education: High school diploma + apprenticeship certification
personal_stake: His grandson's online coding bootcamp depends on reliable low-latency internet, but Frank also fears ISPs are being silenced by Big Tech monopolies—he's genuinely unsure which way to come down.
quirk: Spends 15 hours/week on obscure YouTube channels about 5G towers and banking conspiracies, but is also the go-to guy in his neighborhood for fixing WiFi problems and mentors three young women in electrical trades.

name: Carmen Reyes
education: Associate's degree in nursing, currently pursuing bachelor's online
personal_stake: Her 12-year-old daughter has cystic fibrosis and relies on telemedicine appointments with specialists in Boston; slower or throttled internet speeds during peak hours could literally delay critical care decisions.
quirk: Competes in amateur ultramarathon races (50+ miles) as a way to process stress, which is how she first learned about internet infrastructure—researching GPS watches led her down a rabbit hole of network reliability articles.

name: Marcus Webb
occupation: Commercial real estate agent & amateur competitive birder
education: Bachelor's in Business Administration, some community college coursework in ornithology
personal_stake: My clients—mostly small Black-owned contractors and startups—depend on fast, affordable internet to bid on projects and manage operations from job sites. If ISPs can throttle their bandwidth or charge premium rates, it kills their ability to compete against larger firms who can afford tiered access.
quirk: Spends every Sunday morning before 7am on rare bird documentation across Texas coastal refuges; has discovered three documented sightings of unusual vagrant species that were published in ornithology journals under his field name

name: Marco Velásquez
occupation: Line cook (evening) and food delivery app contractor (daytime)
education: Some college (interrupted); self-taught in network infrastructure via YouTube
personal_stake: As a DACA recipient with precarious employment status, affordable internet access is my only reliable way to build skills for better work and maintain contact with family in Guadalajara. ISP throttling of educational platforms would directly cut into my limited free time to study network administration.
quirk: Obsessively maintains detailed spreadsheets tracking which food delivery apps' algorithms favor him on certain routes and times; has reverse-engineered their rating systems as a hobby.

name: Marcus Chen-Walsh
education: Master's in Special Education, undergraduate degree in Biology
personal_stake: My nonverbal and semi-verbal autistic students rely on specific YouTube creators and adaptive learning videos that smaller channels can't afford paid prioritization—throttling them could literally block their access to communication tools and educational content their families have spent years finding.
quirk: Competes seriously in regional mushroom foraging competitions and has identified 47 species in NYC parks; occasionally references fungi taxonomy in casual conversation

name: Dale Hutchins
education: High school diploma, 2 years community college (took business courses in 1990s)
personal_stake: My grandkid in Denver relies on cheap internet for her coding bootcamp while working nights at a diner—if ISPs throttle video streaming, she can't attend virtual classes. I also stream CB radio archives online and worry companies will prioritize big radio stations over hobbyists like me.
quirk: Maintains a 40-year collection of vintage CB radio equipment in his garage and occasionally broadcasts on legal frequencies; also writes detailed reviews of truck stops on obscure RV forums under an alias.

name: Derek Theriault
education: High school diploma, community college welding certification
personal_stake: His 16-year-old daughter relies on YouTube tutorials and online courses for homeschooling while he's at sea; a two-tier internet would make her educational content prohibitively expensive. He's also frustrated that Comcast throttles his construction crew's video calls to jobsites.
quirk: Restores vintage outboard motors as a side hobby and sells them on eBay; spends more time troubleshooting internet latency issues with engine diagnostics than most people realize are connected

name: Derek Chambliss
occupation: Gas station attendant / part-time online student
education: High school diploma; currently enrolled in community college (online, general studies)
personal_stake: His only access to college coursework depends on streaming video lectures over a throttled mobile hotspot—his ISP already deprioritizes traffic during peak hours. If ISPs start charging course platforms extra for 'fast lane' access, his tuition becomes unaffordable.
quirk: Obsessively restores vintage arcade machines in his garage on weekends; has a 1983 Pac-Man cabinet he's been debugging for 8 months.

name: Deborah Christensen
occupation: Etsy shop owner (handmade educational manipulatives) & homeschool curriculum developer
education: Bachelor's in Mathematics, some online coursework in web design
personal_stake: Her Etsy shop reaches rural homeschool families in 40+ states—if ISPs throttle educational content or charge her small business premium rates to compete with Amazon, she loses her primary income and her ability to fund her six kids' education materials.
quirk: Competes in regional rock climbing competitions on weekends; has a sponsorship deal with a climbing gear company that she's concerned will be deprioritized by her ISP

name: Dorothy Kemp
occupation: Public librarian & senior tech literacy instructor
education: MLIS from University of Michigan, BA in Philosophy
personal_stake: My 73-year-old students—mostly on fixed incomes—can't afford tiered internet speeds to access telehealth appointments and job training. Last month, a student's grandchild's school Zoom access would've required a 'premium' package.
quirk: Competitive tournament Scrabble player who maintains a private 15,000-word glossary of obsolete words; regularly consults it while grading student papers

name: Dr. Marcus Thiel
education: DVM from Iowa State, some online coursework in data science
personal_stake: His practice depends on telemedicine consultations with specialists in Minneapolis and real-time diagnostic uploads from remote ranches; slower or throttled connections literally cost him cattle lives and client trust. ISP bundling deals that prioritize entertainment over medical data transfer feel like a direct threat to his ability to serve.
quirk: Collects vintage ham radio equipment and has rebuilt three non-functional CB radios as a side hobby; sees net neutrality through the lens of communication infrastructure equality that predates the internet.

name: Marcus Chen-O'Sullivan
occupation: Union plumber (Local 12) and part-time esports tournament organizer
education: High school diploma + apprenticeship; currently auditing computer science courses at community college
personal_stake: His esports side gig streams tournaments from a basement studio in Dorchester—ISP throttling during peak hours kills his audience quality and sponsorships. Also mentors at-risk kids from his neighborhood who game online; losing net neutrality would price out the ones on fixed incomes.

name: Dale Hendricks
occupation: Fly fishing guide and outdoor content creator
education: High school diploma, two community college courses in business
personal_stake: My Instagram following of 47k anglers generates 60% of my guide bookings through direct messages and tagged posts. If ISPs can throttle video uploads from rural Montana, I can't post the real-time river condition videos that my clients depend on to book trips.

name: Marco Solis
occupation: Line cook (day shift) and food delivery driver (evenings)
education: Some college (2 years completed before financial constraints)
personal_stake: Marco relies on affordable data to coordinate between two jobs, manage his DACA work authorization documents digitally, and teach himself coding via free YouTube tutorials at night—ISP throttling of video platforms would directly threaten his upskilling efforts and job flexibility.

name: Harpreet Singh Patel
education: High school diploma, 6 months community college (incomplete)
personal_stake: My truck stop's WiFi is a lifeline for drivers stuck overnight—if ISPs throttle streaming or charge fast-lane fees for video calls home, my customers lose their only way to reach families. I've built this business on being a rest stop with dignity, not just fuel.

name: Linh Tran
occupation: Senior Software Engineer at mid-size biotech startup
education: BS Computer Science from San Jose State, self-taught in molecular biology
personal_stake: Her kids use Khan Academy and free coding tutorials that would be throttled under tiered internet service. She's also acutely aware that her own immigrant family's economic mobility depended on equal access to information online.

name: Marcus Chen-Williams
occupation: Tenant rights organizer & part-time aquaponics consultant
education: BA in Philosophy, attended 2 years law school (didn't finish)

quirk: Competes in competitive speed-stacking (cup stacking) at regional tournaments and has a personal YouTube channel about it with 12K subscribers

name: Marcus Webb
education: B.S. in Agricultural Engineering, online coursework in network infrastructure
personal_stake: My farm relies on precision agriculture software and real-time weather data that requires consistent, affordable broadband—if ISPs throttle agricultural tech startups or demand premium rates for precision farming services, my 800 acres become uncompetitive against corporate mega-farms.
quirk: Competes in competitive online gaming tournaments at night using the same fiber connection he uses to run crop modeling software during the day; his esports team name is 'Soil Scientists'.

name: Mara Castellanos
education: Some college (2 years toward BFA, left to transition full-time)
personal_stake: Her illustration portfolio and income depend on uploading large files to niche Discord communities and Patreon; she's terrified ISPs will throttle LGBTQ+ content platforms that don't pay premium rates, effectively making her invisible to clients.
quirk: Obsessively collects vintage coffee equipment from estate sales and has a blog documenting them that gets zero traffic but she updates weekly anyway.

name: Martin Cresswell
education: Master's degree in divinity; self-taught in network infrastructure
personal_stake: My rural congregation of 34 people relies on streaming services and telehealth to survive—slower internet during peak hours means missed Sunday services for shut-ins and lost tithe income we desperately need. If ISPs can throttle religious nonprofits, we're finished.

name: Carmen Reyes
education: RN (Associate's degree) + some community college coursework in environmental science
personal_stake: Her 11-year-old son has cystic fibrosis and relies on low-latency telehealth appointments with specialists in California; she's terrified that ISPs will prioritize video streams over medical data, making his care unaffordable or dangerously delayed.
quirk: Competes in amateur ultramarathon trail running on weekends as a way to process stress; has finished three 50-milers while managing her son's medical needs and full-time nursing shifts.

name: Frank Kowalski
education: High school diploma + apprenticeship certification
personal_stake: His grandson's esports coaching startup can't compete with big streamers who pay ISPs for priority bandwidth. Frank sees this as the same corporate squeezing that killed small union shops in the 80s.
quirk: Spends 4+ hours daily on alternative YouTube channels about 5G towers and Bilderberg Group, but recently became convinced net neutrality is the *only* thing stopping telecom monopolies from becoming a 'digital cartel'—a view he arrived at through conspiracy logic rather than policy analysis.

name: Miriam Rothstein
occupation: Retired librarian and unpublished memoir author
education: Master's in Library Science, undergraduate in History
personal_stake: She's trying to self-publish her mother's Holocaust testimony online and fears ISPs will throttle independent digital archives. Her late mother's story will only survive if the internet remains equally accessible to everyone, rich or poor.
quirk: Obsessively collects vintage typewriter repair manuals and owns 12 working machines; refuses to write on computers but transcribes everything by hand before typing drafts

name: Sophea Ung
education: High school (completed in refugee camp); self-taught in business and technology
personal_stake: My donut shop relies on YouTube tutorials and Instagram to compete with chains—if ISPs throttle small business traffic, I can't afford premium speeds. My two kids stream their college classes from my shop's WiFi, and I need reliable internet to stay afloat.
quirk: Competes in amateur bird photography contests and has won regional awards; keeps detailed spreadsheets of every supplier cost since 1998 when she started the business with $3,000 in savings

name: Marcus Webb
education: High school diploma + Army medical training + self-taught apiculture certification
personal_stake: Marcus sells premium local honey online through a small Etsy shop that depends on reliable, affordable rural broadband to reach customers nationwide. Without net neutrality protections, ISPs could throttle his e-commerce traffic or charge premium rates that would kill his already-thin margins.
quirk: Collects and restores vintage beekeeping equipment from the 1950s-70s; has correspondence with beekeepers in 12 countries via email pen-pal network he's maintained for 8 years

name: Marcus Delgado
occupation: Professional Bee Removal Specialist & Live Honeycomb Relocator
education: High School Diploma + 15 years apprenticeship; self-taught in entomology
personal_stake: I run my business scheduling removals and uploading documentation to cloud storage on a tight budget; if ISPs throttle video uploads or my rural internet gets deprioritized, I can't prove my work to insurance companies or train my apprentices remotely.
quirk: Teaches beekeeping to formerly incarcerated people; has successfully released over 8,000 hives into native ecosystems; keeps detailed journals in Spanish and English that he's digitizing

name: Gerald Hutchins
occupation: Retired USPS mail carrier, now part-time telehealth moderator for rural diabetes clinics
education: High school diploma, 40 hours of medical coding certification (2019)
personal_stake: His rural diabetes patients in Appalachia can't afford tiered internet pricing that would block their access to telemedicine appointments he helps coordinate. He's seen firsthand how slow broadband in poor areas literally costs lives.
quirk: Collects vintage postal uniforms from different eras and has memorized the ZIP codes of every neighborhood he ever walked; sometimes references them unprompted

name: Marcus Delaney
education: High school diploma + military medical training + community college business courses
personal_stake: His beekeeping supply business relies on Zoom consultations with university apiarists and YouTube tutorials to troubleshoot colony collapse; a tiered internet would price him out of the expert networks he depends on to stay competitive with larger operations.
quirk: Competes in regional chainsaw carving competitions and sells wooden bee sculptures at farmers markets; has a 40,000-bee operation named after his late daughter.

name: Marcus Chen-Delgado
occupation: DoorDash driver / amateur competitive Pokémon card grader
education: High school diploma, currently enrolled in CompTIA A+ prep course via YouTube
personal_stake: I deliver food using my phone and throttled data speeds eat my earnings—literally lose delivery orders when the network lags. ISPs could pay DoorDash to deprioritize my competitors' apps, killing my side gig income that pays for tech school tuition.
quirk: Spends 8 hours weekly photographing and documenting 1990s Pokémon card conditions for online authentication; maintains a meticulously organized binder that he drives around Las Vegas in his DoorDash vehicle

name: Marcus Okonkwo
education: MBA in Finance, undergraduate in Philosophy
personal_stake: His teenage daughter's indie music streaming startup relies on affordable bandwidth to compete against Spotify; he's also concerned that ISP throttling could create the same information asymmetries that destabilized markets in 2008.
quirk: Spends weekends restoring vintage synthesizers and blogs anonymously about behavioral economics under the handle 'ArbitrageOfIdeas' with 847 subscribers.

name: Marcus Holloway
occupation: Bee removal specialist and part-time beekeeper
education: High school diploma, extensive self-taught entomology study
personal_stake: I stream my hive monitoring setup on Twitch to help other beekeepers—throttling my upload speed would cripple my ability to share real-time hive health data with beekeepers across the country who rely on it for colony management.
quirk: Competes in competitive speedrunning communities online despite having arthritic hands; has a following of 12,000 people who watch him beat 30-year-old games in unconventional ways

name: Marcus DeWitt
education: Bachelor's in Music Performance, some community college coursework in IT
personal_stake: He teaches piano lessons via Zoom to 12 students nationwide—three are low-income kids in rural areas where ISPs throttle video. His chronic back pain means he can't travel to gigs, so affordable reliable broadband is literally his livelihood.
quirk: Spends weekends reverse-engineering old MIDI synthesizers and posting detailed repair tutorials on a niche YouTube channel that has 8,000 subscribers; uses this income to offset medical copays.

name: Marcus Webb
education: High school diploma + military technical training
personal_stake: My eBay business margins are razor-thin, and I depend on consistent upload speeds to photograph inventory and ship listings on time. If ISPs can throttle small sellers while fast-tracking Amazon Prime, I lose my only reliable income source.
quirk: Compulsively restores vintage CB radios and broadcasts under a fake handle about obscure Detroit radio history; has 47 followers on a niche podcast nobody knows about

name: Marcus Webb
occupation: Retired USPS mail carrier, part-time competitive axe-throwing instructor
education: High school diploma, community college courses in small business management
personal_stake: His daughter runs a small telemedicine startup from her apartment that helps rural diabetics like him monitor blood sugar remotely; she'll be crushed if ISPs can throttle her bandwidth unless she pays extortion rates.
quirk: Obsessively documents cloud formations with a 1970s film camera and sells the prints at farmers markets; has never owned a smartphone, only a basic flip phone and a desktop computer.

name: Marcus Chen-Williams
education: Some college (2 years toward engineering degree before enlisting)
personal_stake: His eBay vintage audio equipment business depends on reliable upload speeds to post listings and videos; throttling would kill his ability to compete against Amazon sellers with corporate resources.
quirk: Collects and restores 1970s synthesizers as therapy—has 12 in various states of repair in his apartment and occasionally sells recordings of them on Bandcamp for $2-5.

name: Derek Christiansen
education: BBA Finance, University of Utah; self-taught in network infrastructure through hobby
personal_stake: His three teenagers rely on open internet access for homeschool co-ops and online music lessons; he's seen ISPs throttle educational streaming in his area and fears rural Mormon communities will be priced out of broadband entirely.
quirk: Competes in competitive online chess tournaments under an anonymous handle and has maintained a top-1000 USCF rating for eight years without telling his family.

name: Marcus Delgado
education: High school diploma + vocational trade certifications
personal_stake: He relies on YouTube tutorials to train younger workers on safety protocols and new techniques, and needs affordable consistent internet access across job sites. His union's organizing efforts depend on Facebook groups and encrypted messaging apps that could be throttled by ISPs favoring corporate communications.
quirk: Competitive ballroom dancer who practices salsa every Thursday night and once placed second in a regional amateur competition

name: Deborah Christensen
occupation: Etsy shop owner (handmade educational materials) & part-time medical coder
education: Associate's degree in business; self-taught in HTML/CSS
personal_stake: Her Etsy shop's competitive advantage depends on equal bandwidth access to reach rural homeschooling families; she's also terrified that ISP throttling could slow the medical coding work she does nights, jeopardizing her family's health insurance.
quirk: She's an enthusiast of obsolete technology—collects working Commodore 64s and writes blog posts comparing retro computing ethics to modern internet fairness issues, which surprisingly gets 2K monthly readers.

name: Marcus Webb
education: MBA in Finance, undergrad in Philosophy
personal_stake: His teenage daughter's indie music production startup needs affordable cloud bandwidth to compete with major labels' infrastructure; he's watched ISP throttling destroy smaller ventures' competitive advantage in other sectors.
quirk: Collects vintage synthesizers and maintains a YouTube channel (12K subscribers) reviewing 1980s electronic music gear under the pseudonym 'SynthArchaeology'—he sees net neutrality through the lens of preserving access for creative hobbyists like himself.

name: Marcus Chen
occupation: UC Santa Barbara marine biology student + part-time aquarium tank maintenance technician
education: Currently enrolled, junior standing, 3.1 GPA
personal_stake: My coastal research lab depends on reliable, unthrottled internet to upload real-time kelp forest sensor data to collaborators in Chile and Japan—if ISPs prioritize some data over others, our $80K NSF grant becomes worthless. Plus, I'm addicted to Discord marine biology servers at 2am.
quirk: Competes in competitive eating contests on weekends (holds regional record for oyster shucking) while maintaining a strict ethical stance against overfishing in his academic work

name: Marcus Thorne
education: High school diploma + 2 years community college (unfinished), self-taught networking hobbyist
personal_stake: My nephew in rural Mingo County relies on free YouTube coding tutorials via public WiFi to learn programming—his only escape route from coal country. ISP throttling of video would literally erase his future.
quirk: Maintains an obsessive spreadsheet tracking download speeds at different times/locations across West Virginia; has been doing this for 4 years as an unpaid hobby

name: Margaret Talayesva
occupation: Retired weaver and online craft marketplace seller (uses library WiFi)
education: High school diploma + self-taught e-commerce
personal_stake: My granddaughter helps me list traditional Hopi textiles online to earn supplemental income, but our 7-mile drive to the library for reliable internet makes this increasingly unsustainable. ISPs are prioritizing wealthy zip codes while my community gets ignored—net neutrality is the only thing preventing them from throttling my sales platform entirely.
quirk: Competes in online Scrabble tournaments despite having never owned a personal computer; plays exclusively at the community center against opponents in Japan and South Africa

name: Margaret Chen-Kowalski
occupation: Organic biodynamic farmer & amateur radio enthusiast
education: High school diploma + self-taught via shortwave radio manuals and ARRL courses
personal_stake: My farm's broadband keeps throttling when I stream soil moisture sensor data to my co-op's shared cloud drive, and rural ISPs already ignore us—net neutrality is the only thing keeping them from charging me extra for agricultural data. Also my grandkids can't do homework here without paying premium rates.
quirk: Hosts an unlicensed 40-meter radio net every Tuesday evening where she discusses microbiome theory and conspiracy theories with listeners across three continents, refuses to use email but will send faxes

name: Margaret Taho
education: Associate's degree (nursing), self-taught in traditional textile restoration
personal_stake: My granddaughter uses the library's wifi to attend college online—if ISPs throttle educational traffic, she loses access to the only path forward her parents could afford. I'm also selling weavings nationally through a co-op's website and need affordable connectivity to compete with overseas sellers.
quirk: Competes in competitive online Scrabble tournaments at the library on Tuesday afternoons despite having dial-up at home; ranked 14th in Arizona for her age group.

name: Marcus Delgado
education: High school diploma, 2 years community college (business admin, incomplete)
personal_stake: His union is trying to use fast, affordable internet to stream training videos and coordinate job sites across the Southwest; Comcast keeps throttling their uploads, forcing them to pay premium tiers they can't afford.
quirk: Competitive competitive eater on weekends—has placed in three regional jalapeño-eating contests; his competitive eating community organizes entirely through Discord.

name: Patricia Mewborn
occupation: Retired bank teller / volunteer church livestream operator
education: High school diploma + community college accounting courses
personal_stake: My church's YouTube channel reaches homebound elderly across three counties who can't attend in person. If ISPs throttle religious content or demand fees, I'll have to shut down the only spiritual connection these people have.
quirk: Collects and restores vintage ham radios as a hobby; has 12 working units from the 1970s-80s and teaches a monthly class on radio repair at the community center

name: Marcus Chen-Whitmore
occupation: Machine Learning Researcher at autonomous systems startup
education: PhD in Computer Science (Stanford), but dropped out of high school once
personal_stake: My startup trains vision models on massive distributed datasets that would be economically unviable if ISPs throttle ML training traffic or create paid fast lanes for competitors' data pipelines. Also, I grew up poor with unreliable internet and I'm philosophically opposed to digital class systems.
quirk: Competes in semi-professional ballroom dancing competitions on weekends; has placed third regionally in Latin category

name: Dale Kemper
education: High school diploma, community college diesel mechanics (incomplete)
personal_stake: His daughter runs a small e-commerce business selling vintage CB radio parts from her garage in Nebraska; she can't compete with Amazon because ISPs throttle her traffic in rural areas. He's also losing CB radio culture and sees net neutrality as preventing corporate gatekeeping.
quirk: Collects and restores 1970s CB radios as a side hobby; has a working setup in his truck cab and still uses it to communicate with other drivers despite owning a smartphone.

name: Darius Chen
education: Some college (community college, coursework in statistics incomplete)
personal_stake: Payment processors routinely reject or freeze accounts for sex workers, forcing reliance on sketchy third-party platforms that throttle bandwidth or sell data. Net neutrality rules prevent ISPs from creating similar discrimination bottlenecks that could completely eliminate my ability to advertise or communicate with clients.
quirk: Volunteers as a translator for Mandarin-speaking clients at a local legal aid clinic three afternoons a week, partly to stay grounded in non-sex-work community

name: Derek Sorensen
education: MBA in Finance, Brigham Young University
personal_stake: His oldest daughter runs a small handmade-jewelry Etsy shop that relies on equal bandwidth access to compete with larger sellers; he's also deeply uncomfortable with corporate gatekeeping on moral grounds tied to his faith's emphasis on stewardship.
quirk: Obsessively restores vintage ham radios as a hobby and genuinely prefers communicating over 40-meter band to texting, which makes him acutely aware of infrastructure access issues despite being financially comfortable.

name: Margaret Chen-Kowalski
education: MD from University of Pennsylvania, Board-certified in Diagnostic Radiology
personal_stake: I read CT scans for 14 rural hospitals in Appalachia and the Great Plains via telehealth—my diagnoses depend on reliable, unthrottled bandwidth to transmit high-resolution imaging without lag. If ISPs can prioritize traffic, rural ERs will get deprioritized, literally delaying stroke and heart attack diagnoses.
quirk: Competitive ballroom dancer who trains 10 hours weekly; once won a regional tango championship while on-call for emergencies

name: Carlos Mejía-Chen
occupation: Mechanical engineer (aerospace turbine design), part-time emergency preparedness consultant
education: BS Mechanical Engineering (UPRM), MS Systems Engineering (online, Georgia Tech)
personal_stake: During Hurricane Maria I coordinated disaster response via internet because cell towers failed—fast, unthrottled access to engineering forums and real-time satellite data saved lives in my family's neighborhood. ISP throttling during emergencies could literally kill people in the next disaster.
quirk: Obsessively rebuilds vintage Honda motorcycles as therapy; has 3 incomplete bikes in his garage and livestreams restoration work to a niche YouTube audience of 12K subscribers, mostly other engineers

name: Marcus Webb
occupation: TikTok content creator / part-time medical coder
education: High school diploma + online coding bootcamp (incomplete)
personal_stake: ISP throttling is killing my music promotion strategy—my beats take forever to upload on slower lanes, while major labels' content streams instantly. Without net neutrality, I literally can't compete on equal footing.
quirk: He's obsessed with medical billing software and spent last summer teaching his grandmother how to file insurance claims; his rap persona is 'Dr. Dose' but he's genuinely interested in healthcare IT

name: Margaret Chen-Kowalski
occupation: Retired high school biology teacher, organic dairy farmer (part-time)
education: Master's degree in Environmental Science
personal_stake: My farm uses precision agriculture software and remote monitoring that only works reliably on open internet—if ISPs can throttle data for competing farm tech companies, I'll be forced back to outdated methods. Plus, my grandkids need net neutrality to escape the rural isolation I experienced growing up.
quirk: Competes in regional disc golf tournaments and has named all 47 of her dairy cows after 20th-century women mathematicians

name: Mei Chen
education: Some college (2 years design school, self-taught afterward)
personal_stake: My illustration clients are scattered across rural areas with terrible ISP options—net neutrality protections mean they can actually afford to download my files and pay me without ISPs throttling creative work. Without it, I'm essentially locked out of serving anyone outside major metros.
quirk: Obsessively collects defunct dial-up modems and writes detailed reviews of them on a niche blog that somehow has 3,200 followers

name: Kenji Matsuda
occupation: Retired nursery owner, part-time bonsai restoration consultant
education: High school diploma, extensive self-taught horticulture and Japanese aesthetics
personal_stake: His granddaughter relies on affordable broadband to attend UC Davis engineering classes remotely from his property. He fears ISPs will throttle educational content, recreating the access barriers his parents faced in the internment camps.
quirk: Maintains a decades-long correspondence with a botanist in Kyoto entirely via handwritten letters, refuses email, but recently learned to video-call his granddaughter to identify rare plant specimens together.

name: Marcus Chen
occupation: Independent escort & part-time marine biology researcher
education: Some college (2 years toward BS in Biology, paused)
personal_stake: Payment processors constantly kill my access to standard internet services because of my legal sex work, forcing me onto slower, shadier networks. Net neutrality protections are literally the only thing preventing ISPs from throttling my ability to reach clients and maintain financial independence.
quirk: Spends 15+ hours weekly volunteering at a nonprofit coral restoration lab and dreams of pivoting fully into marine conservation once sex work becomes financially unnecessary.

name: Harpreet Singh Dhaliwal
education: High school diploma, self-taught in networking and logistics software
personal_stake: My truck stop relies on real-time GPS, weather data, and driver communication apps—if ISPs can throttle logistics traffic to favor competitors, small operators like me get crushed. My drivers depend on equal internet access to find routes and report mechanical issues.
quirk: Volunteers as a volunteer firefighter and has memorized the trucking regulations of all 48 continental states

name: Mariah Chen-Kowalski
occupation: Freelance medical transcriptionist and goat dairy operator
education: Some college (2 years, paused for pregnancy)
personal_stake: Her husband deploys for months at a time and relies on affordable, consistent internet to video call their infant daughter. ISP throttling of video platforms would make those calls unusable during his Coast Guard missions.
quirk: Teaches herself obscure programming languages at 3 AM during nursing sessions and maintains a detailed spreadsheet of every open-source project she's contributed to, color-coded by programming language.
